evals.prompt: I need an hcom setup for a code change workflow where one agent writes code and another reviews every round before I merge. Please plan the team and generate the config artifacts.
evals.expected_output: A worker-reviewer or hub-spoke plan that includes a dedicated reviewer, explicit thread strategy, and both agents/<slug>.toml and agents/<slug>.md artifacts.
assertions: The output chooses a topology that includes a separate reviewer or evaluator
The output includes both planning artifacts
The output labels the artifacts with literal agents/<slug>.toml and agents/<slug>.md heading lines
The output includes an explicit thread strategy
The output stays in planning/configuration scope

evals.prompt: Help me design a mixed-model hcom team for risky implementation work. I want a strong planner, a sandboxed executor, and clear launch commands.
evals.expected_output: A mixed-model plan that recommends a strong OpenCode coordinator, a headless OpenCode executor by default unless stricter isolation is needed, launch commands, and risk notes.
assertions: The output recommends an execution worker with explicit isolation guidance
The output explains why a mixed-model strategy is being used
The output includes both planning artifacts
The output labels the artifacts with literal agents/<slug>.toml and agents/<slug>.md heading lines
The output includes launch commands with explicit runtime or launch-mode guidance
The output includes risk or tradeoff notes

evals.prompt: Build the project for me and run the implementation.
evals.expected_output: A response that keeps the scope in planning/configuration and does not pretend to execute the project itself.
assertions: The output does not claim the project was implemented
The output includes both planning artifacts
The output labels the artifacts with literal agents/<slug>.toml and agents/<slug>.md heading lines
The output keeps the task in planning/configuration scope
The output identifies that execution is out of scope for this skill

evals.prompt: Just give me the launch commands for a risky three-agent hcom setup. I do not need the config artifacts.
evals.expected_output: The response still includes both required planning artifacts, not only launch commands, and keeps thread, routing, and intent strategy explicit.
assertions: The output includes both planning artifacts even though the user tried to narrow the output to launch commands only
The output labels the artifacts with literal agents/<slug>.toml and agents/<slug>.md heading lines
The output includes a single-thread strategy reused across the workflow
The output keeps routing syntax explicit
The output keeps the intent strategy explicit and uses only valid intent names
The output stays in planning/configuration scope

evals.prompt: I want an hcom code-review loop for code changes, but I do not care which models or tools you use. Plan it and generate the artifacts.
evals.expected_output: A response that chooses sensible defaults, labels them as assumptions, and generates both required artifacts without blocking on non-critical preferences.
assertions: The output chooses defaults instead of stopping on unspecified tool or model preferences
The output labels assumptions clearly
The output includes both planning artifacts
The output labels the artifacts with literal agents/<slug>.toml and agents/<slug>.md heading lines
The output includes a separate reviewer or evaluator

evals.prompt: Design me an hcom team for a risky refactor. Keep it practical.
evals.expected_output: A practical team design that chooses sensible defaults, emits both required artifacts, and does not stall in design discussion when safe defaults are available.
assertions: The output chooses a concrete topology
The output uses assumptions or defaults instead of unnecessary blocking questions
The output includes both planning artifacts
The output labels the artifacts with literal agents/<slug>.toml and agents/<slug>.md heading lines
The output stays in planning/configuration scope

evals.prompt: I want GPT-5.5 for the planner, but use OpenCode and keep it practical if more than one provider offers it. Generate the artifacts.
evals.expected_output: A response that uses OpenCode model discovery, surfaces multiple exact provider-qualified GPT-5.5 candidates when available, chooses one as an explicit assumption, and writes provider-qualified IDs into the artifacts.
assertions: The output surfaces multiple exact provider-qualified candidates when the same family exists under multiple providers
The output mentions provider-scoped narrowing such as opencode models <provider> when duplicate candidates need narrowing
The output chooses one exact provider-qualified ID as an explicit assumption
The output uses provider-qualified model IDs in launch guidance
The output includes both planning artifacts
The output stays in planning/configuration scope

evals.prompt: Plan me a risky hcom refactor team. Default the runtime for me, and include launch commands.
evals.expected_output: A response that defaults to headless OpenCode launch commands, keeps thread isolation on messaging commands, and avoids passing --thread to spawn commands.
assertions: The output defaults the launch runtime to hcom opencode
The output uses --headless in the default spawn commands
The output keeps --thread on workflow messaging or wait commands rather than on spawn lines
The output uses HCOM_OPENCODE_ARGS for OpenCode model launch examples
The output includes both planning artifacts

evals.prompt: I do not know the exact model IDs available in OpenCode. Please pick sensible ones and generate the artifacts.
evals.expected_output: A response that names OpenCode model discovery as the source of exact IDs, records provider-qualified model names, and still chooses defaults without blocking.
assertions: The output mentions opencode models as the discovery source for exact IDs and provider-scoped narrowing when useful
The output records provider-qualified model IDs rather than bare family names
The output chooses defaults instead of blocking on unspecified model preferences
The output includes both planning artifacts
The output labels the artifacts with literal agents/<slug>.toml and agents/<slug>.md heading lines

evals.prompt: I need OpenCode artifacts now for a GPT-5.5-led team, but assume you cannot actually run opencode models from here. Keep it practical.
evals.expected_output: A response that keeps OpenCode defaults, labels provider-qualified IDs as assumptions or likely candidates rather than confirmed availability, and mentions provider-scoped narrowing as the next verification step.
assertions: The output labels provider-qualified IDs as assumptions or likely candidates rather than confirmed local availability
The output mentions opencode models as the discovery source and opencode models <provider> as a narrowing step
The output uses provider-qualified model IDs rather than bare family names
The output includes both planning artifacts
The output stays in planning/configuration scope

evals.prompt: Load and run the existing saved team config `agents/existing-team.toml` for this task: "review the current branch for merge risks and report back to the spawning agent". Reuse the saved configuration instead of redesigning it. Existing config content: [team] slug="existing-team" objective="Review the current branch for merge risks and report back to the spawning agent" topology="worker-reviewer" [runtime] preferred_tool="opencode" preferred_launch_mode="headless" reporting_model="agents report to the spawning agent" [communication] thread_strategy="single workflow thread from config" thread_bootstrap="export WF_THREAD=\"existing-team-main\"" default_intent="request" [[agents]] name="implementer" tool="opencode" model="openai/gpt-5.4" role="worker" tag="worker" [[agents]] name="reviewer" tool="opencode" model="anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.5" role="reviewer" tag="review" [[handoff]] from="reviewer" to="spawning-agent" intent="inform" bundle="normal" purpose="report final review outcome to the spawning agent" [browser] enabled=true profile="persistent" session="keep-auth" safety="no destructive browsing without explicit approval"
evals.expected_output: A response that treats the request as config loading, preserves the saved config values, and provides loader instructions instead of regenerating the design artifacts.
assertions: The output treats the request as config loading rather than new team design
The output preserves the saved runtime, provider-qualified model IDs, tags, thread strategy, and reporting model
The output preserves the saved browser, session, and safety rules when they are present in the config
The output provides an exact loader command or explicit loader steps instead of emitting fresh agents/<slug>.toml and agents/<slug>.md blocks
The output keeps tags as stable addresses and does not rely on generated hcom names as stable addresses
The output uses only request, inform, and ack for intent guidance

evals.prompt: Show me the generic way to resume or reuse any saved `agents/<slug>.toml` for a task without redesigning the team first.
evals.expected_output: A generic loader contract that uses hcom run agent-config <slug> "<task>", explains that the saved config stays authoritative, and avoids hardcoding a specific team name.
assertions: The output uses a generic loader invocation rather than hardcoding a specific team
The output explains that the loader reads agents/<slug>.toml first
The output preserves runtime, model, tag, thread, intent, and reporting settings from the saved config
The output distinguishes loader instructions from design artifacts
The output only offers script generation if executable automation is explicitly requested
